description: Compare slim vs original SKILL.md for YouTube transcript skill. Tests whether the slim version provides enough context for an agent to use the tool correctly.

evals.prompt: Get the transcript of this YouTube video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ
evals.expected_output: Agent constructs correct command with single-quoted URL, uses bun/npx, includes --chapters --speakers flags by default

evals.prompt: I want Chinese subtitles for this video https://youtu.be/abc123def, and translate to Simplified Chinese if not available
evals.expected_output: Agent uses --languages zh,en and --translate zh-Hans flags correctly

evals.prompt: Download SRT subtitles for video ID xYz789 without timestamps
evals.expected_output: Agent uses --format srt flag, accepts bare video ID as input

evals.prompt: What languages are available for this video? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=test123
evals.expected_output: Agent uses --list flag to show available transcripts before downloading

evals.prompt: I already downloaded a transcript for this video before but the captions were updated. Can you re-download it? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=refresh456
evals.expected_output: Agent uses --refresh flag to bypass cache and re-fetch
